Vehicles.VehicleBuildDef.label: Cierva C30
Vehicles.VehicleBuildDef.description:
An early rotary-wing aircraft developed by Juan de la Cierva that used an unpowered rotor for lift and a conventional propeller for thrust. Safer and more stable than fixed-wing aircraft at low speeds, it was an important step toward modern helicopter design.

Being a flying vehicle, it is extremely obvious when on the move.

<color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Pilot x1, Passenger x1
<color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Chemfuel
